title: Base-ten blocks generator (unit/rod/flat + thousand-cube net)
context: Base-ten blocks (units, rods of 10, flats of 100, and thousand cubes) are the canonical manipulative for place-value understanding. Their relational invariant is strict: 10 units must exactly equal 1 rod in every dimension; 10 rods must exactly equal 1 flat; 10 flats must exactly equal the thousand-cube net face. A printable base-ten block set that violates these relationships teaches a false understanding of place value. The thousand cube is printed as a net (6-face unfold) that can be cut and folded.
objective: Implement a base-ten blocks generator module that produces units, rods, flats, and a thousand-cube net with geometrically exact 10:1 relationships, verified by the harness.
acceptanceCriteria: Generates unit squares, rod (strip of 10 units), flat (10x10 grid of units), and thousand-cube net (6-face unfold).
Relationship invariants pass verification: 10 units == 1 rod; 10 rods == 1 flat; flat dimensions match the thousand-cube net face, all within style-guide tolerance.
Includes grid lines on rods and flats to show individual units, legible in grayscale.
Thousand-cube net includes fold/cut guide lines and is labelled '1000'.
Generates all standard variants: A4/Letter x colour/grayscale/ink-saver x standard/large-print.
MIT-licensed; all commits DCO-signed; passes pnpm build, pnpm test, and CI verification gate.
resources: tasks/math-manip-code-002.json -- verification harness (must extend to check 10:1 relationships)
output: A base-ten blocks generator module integrated into the generator engine package, with a spec file (specs/base-ten-blocks.spec.json) and example output PDFs, in a pull request, MIT-licensed.
